message:
build(contracts): pin asupersync to crates.io 0.3.0, drop git+rev contract fields

Previous Cargo contract for `asupersync` required:
  git = "https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync"
  rev = "310ff61f"
  version = "0.2.9"

The 0.3.0 migration on the asupersync side unified every consumer of
the crate — the direct dependency here, the frankensqlite transitive,
and the frankensearch transitive — onto a single crates.io-published
release. Keeping the git+rev pin in place would force a mismatch any
time one of those transitives rev-bumps, even though the published
0.3.0 ABI is identical across all of them.

Changes in `CONTRACTS`:

  asupersync entry:
    - expected_git    = "https://github.com/.../asupersync"  →  ""
    - expected_rev    = "310ff61f"                           →  ""
    - expected_version = "0.2.9"                             →  "0.3.0"

The `validate_manifest_dependency_spec()` helper is extended to treat
empty `expected_git` as "skip git/rev contract checks for this dep"
so the contract engine does not blow up on the now-stripped fields.
Non-empty `expected_git` entries continue to enforce git+rev as
before, so every other contract in the table is unaffected.

Net effect: `cargo build` stops complaining about the asupersync
dependency shape as long as the manifest has `asupersync = { version
= "0.3.0", features = ["test-internals", "tls-native-roots"] }` (or
an equivalent table form), and does not look at git/rev at all.

No runtime code change. Build-script only.
